title: Shadira Gordon, DVM, MPVM
text:
About Me

I am a graduate student researcher working at the Center for Animal Disease Modeling and Surveillance (CADMS) at UC Davis. My work bridges veterinary medicine, data science, and public health through a One Health lens.
My research focuses on disease surveillance, infectious disease modeling, and antimicrobial resistance. I build interactive data tools, including R Shiny dashboards for disease surveillance and livestock health monitoring, that turn complex epidemiological data into actionable insights for researchers, producers, government agencies, and other organizations.
I’m deeply interested in developing strategies for emerging zoonotic threats preparedness, improving global disease surveillance systems, and preventing and controlling infectious diseases through a One Health approach.



Highlights

Toolkit: R Studio, Python, ArcGIS, QGIS, SQL, Apache Spark, etc.
Research: Observational studies, AMR surveillance, outbreak investigation
Teaching: Disease ecology, infectious disease modeling, epidemiology course content development, etc.
